questions.q: According to an April 14, 2026 report, MRI scans have revealed that diets high in ultraprocessed foods are physically altering human bodies in what surprising way?
options: Turning thighs into well-marbled steaks with intramuscular fat
Decreasing the density of the frontal lobe
Tinting fingernails a pale yellow color
Creating permanent plaque coatings on the tongue
questions.correctText: Turning thighs into well-marbled steaks with intramuscular fat

questions.q: Why did several US states begin dropping Medicaid coverage for popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs in mid-April 2026?
options: An unsustainable rise in costs and public spending due to high demand
A federal mandate banning the off-label prescription of the drugs
Recent FDA warnings linking the medications to severe liver failure
A severe supply chain shortage that halted domestic manufacturing
questions.correctText: An unsustainable rise in costs and public spending due to high demand

questions.q: According to a joint investigation by USA TODAY published on April 14, 2026, what major tactic has Merck used to keep the price of its blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda sky high?
options: Using a growing wall of patents to delay generic competition
Artificially limiting global production to create market scarcity
Paying kickbacks to oncologists who refuse to prescribe alternatives
Monopolizing the supply chain for a rare active ingredient
questions.correctText: Using a growing wall of patents to delay generic competition

questions.q: Despite the US flu season largely coming to an end, what alarming health trend was the CDC still tracking as of April 14, 2026?
options: The number of child flu deaths is continuing to rise
A new strain of the flu is severely reinfecting vaccinated adults
Hospitals are reporting a massive shortage of rapid diagnostic flu tests
The virus is unexpectedly spreading through municipal water supplies
questions.correctText: The number of child flu deaths is continuing to rise

questions.q: A prominent April 14, 2026 NPR report focused on the growing trend of consumers ordering their own blood work. What was the primary issue highlighted with this practice?
options: Interpreting the results often raises more questions for patients than it can answer
Insurance companies are using the data to unlawfully deny coverage
State medical boards are aggressively banning direct-to-consumer lab testing
At-home samples are frequently cross-contaminated while in transit
questions.correctText: Interpreting the results often raises more questions for patients than it can answer
